steps.title: Render the operator greeting
steps.intent: Display a greeting line addressed to the operator when the view loads.
steps.acceptance: The rendered view contains the approved greeting string addressed to the operator.
steps.stand_in: Greeting text "Hello, operator!" (carried over from the demo3 precedent plan) pending q-greeting-text.

steps.title: Display a live local-time clock
steps.intent: Show the current wall-clock time in the operator's local timezone alongside the greeting, refreshing so it stays current.
steps.acceptance: The rendered view shows the current time in the chosen local timezone and the displayed value advances within 2 seconds.
steps.stand_in: Browser-local timezone via Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone, 24-hour HH:MM:SS format, refreshing every second — pending q-timezone and q-clock-format.

verification_targets.check: The rendered greeting string equals the operator-approved greeting text exactly.
The displayed time equals the current time in the approved timezone, within a 60-second tolerance.
The displayed clock value changes at least once within any 2-second observation window.

boundary_cases.description: At the day boundary the clock rolls over correctly (23:59:59 advances to 00:00:00) in the chosen timezone.
When the local timezone cannot be resolved (Intl unavailable or no configured zone), the view refuses with a setup hint rather than silently falling back to UTC.
Across a daylight-saving transition in the chosen timezone the displayed wall-clock time stays correct (no duplicated or skipped hour on screen).
